name: Italy
contextBlurb: Italy applies the EU MiCA framework to stablecoins. Euro-pegged e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) may be issued or offered to retail customers when authorised under MiCA and relevant national laws. Italy has designated the Bank of Italy (prudential and EMI oversight) and CONSOB (conduct and CASP oversight) as competent authorities. MiCA's stablecoin rules have applied since 30 June 2024 and CASP rules since 30 December 2024, with Italy providing a national transition (application deadline extended to 30 December 2025 and transitional activity possible until 30 June 2026 for eligible firms).

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
MiCA CASP rules apply EU-wide (with national transitions)
EBA publishes Final Guidelines on redemption plans for ART/EMT issuers (orderly redemption and crisis-management expectations)

keyFigures.name: Bank of Italy
keyFigures.role: Primary supervisor for stablecoin issuers, focusing on financial stability, systemic risk, and cross-border issuance con

keyFigures.name: CONSOB
keyFigures.role: Oversees securities-related aspects of crypto-assets, ensuring investor protection and compliance with MiCAR disclosure

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Italy applies the EU MiCA framework to stablecoins. Euro-pegged e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) may be issued or offered to retail customers when authorised under MiCA and relevant national laws. Italy has designated the Bank of Italy (prudential and EMI oversight) and CONSOB (conduct and CASP oversight) as competent authorities. MiCA's stablecoin rules have applied since 30 June 2024 and CASP rules since 30 December 2024, with Italy providing a national transition (application deadline extended to 30 December 2025 and transitional activity possible until 30 June 2026 for eligible firms).
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Under MiCAR, stablecoins are classified as crypto-assets, specifically as e-money tokens (EMTs) or asset-referenced tokens (ARTs), and are not considered legal tender.

regulators.name: Bank of Italy
regulators.role: Primary supervisor for stablecoin issuers, focusing on financial stability, systemic risk, and cross-border issuance concerns, especially regarding multi-issuance models.

regulators.name: CONSOB
regulators.role: Oversees securities-related aspects of crypto-assets, ensuring investor protection and compliance with MiCAR disclosure rules.
